sourceMovie.title: Body Double
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Body Double for the same reason it broke through in 1984: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Brian De Palma keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chinatown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Body Double clicked for you, Chinatown (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Body Double?
faq.answer: Try Chinatown, Blue Velvet, Kiss Kiss Bang Bang—each shares DNA with Body Double while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Body Double?
faq.answer: Body Double (1984) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Body Double worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
